title: Install AEM Martech Plugin
description: Integrate Adobe marketing technology into your AEM Edge Delivery Services project using the AEM Martech plugin. This plan decomposes monolithic Adobe Launch into phased lifecycle components — eager (personalization), lazy (analytics), and delayed (third-party tags) — powered by Adobe Web SDK (Alloy).

tasks.title: Verify prerequisites and Launch container configuration
tasks.description:
Before instrumenting the project, confirm the following prerequisites are met.

**Required access:**
- **Adobe Experience Platform** (basic permissions for data collection)
- **Adobe Analytics**
- **Adobe Target** or **Adobe Journey Optimizer**

**Launch container configuration (critical):**
- **DO NOT** include these extensions in your Launch container — the plugin handles them directly:
  - `Adobe Experience Platform Web SDK`
  - `Adobe Analytics`
  - `Adobe Target`
- **DO** ensure the `Adobe Client Data Layer` extension is configured

Including the above extensions in Launch will lead to conflicts and potential data duplication.

**Consent disclaimer:** This library defaults user consent to `pending`. Setting consent to `in` grants consent by default without explicit user agreement. Consult legal counsel regarding privacy obligations and appropriate configuration.

tasks.title: Add preload and preconnect hints to head.html
tasks.description:
Add three resource hints at the end of `head.html` to enable early browser fetching without blocking rendering:

- `<link rel="preload" as="script" crossorigin="anonymous" href="/plugins/martech/src/index.js"/>`
- `<link rel="preload" as="script" crossorigin="anonymous" href="/plugins/martech/src/alloy.min.js"/>`
- `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://edge.adobedc.net"/>`

These preload the plugin scripts and establish an early connection to Adobe Edge Network.

tasks.title: Import martech functions in scripts.js
tasks.description:
Add the following import at the top of `scripts/scripts.js` with the other imports:

```js
import { initMartech, updateUserConsent, martechEager, martechLazy, martechDelayed } from '../plugins/martech/src/index.js';
```

**Note:** The path is relative from the `scripts/` directory to the `plugins/` directory.

tasks.title: Add martech configuration and wire initMartech into loadEager
tasks.description:
In `scripts/scripts.js`, call `initMartech` at the top of the `loadEager` function with two arguments:

**1. WebSDK configuration object:**
- `datastreamId` — set to `'DATASTREAM_ID_HERE'` (placeholder)
- `orgId` — set to `'IMS_ORG_ID_HERE'` (placeholder)
- `onBeforeEventSend` — optional callback to modify XDM payload before send
- `edgeConfigOverrides` — optional datastream overrides for different environments

**2. Library configuration object:**
- `personalization` — gate with `!!getMetadata('target') && isConsentGiven`
- `launchUrls` — set to empty array `[]`

Store the returned promise in a variable called `martechLoadedPromise`. The placeholder values for `datastreamId` and `orgId` will be replaced by the user in a later task.

tasks.title: Wait for personalization in loadEager
tasks.description:
In the `loadEager` function of `scripts/scripts.js`, modify the section-loading block inside `if (main)` to run personalization alongside the first section render.

**Place this after:**
- `decorateMain(main)`
- `document.body.classList.add('appear')`

**Use this pattern:**
```js
await Promise.all([
  martechLoadedPromise.then(martechEager),
  loadSection(main.querySelector('.section'), waitForFirstImage),
]);
```

This runs personalization and first-section loading in parallel, preventing flash of original content (FOOC) when personalization is enabled.

tasks.title: Add martechLazy call to loadLazy function
tasks.description:
In the `loadLazy` function of `scripts/scripts.js`, add `await martechLazy()` after the existing lazy-loading logic (e.g. after `loadFooter`).

**Requirements:**
- Triggers analytics beacon setup and fires deferred analytics events
- **Must** be awaited independently — do **not** wrap in `Promise.all` with other operations
- Sequential processing is required for correct event handling

tasks.title: Add martechDelayed call to loadDelayed function
tasks.description:
In the `loadDelayed` function of `scripts/scripts.js`, add `martechDelayed()` within the existing `setTimeout` wrapper alongside other delayed operations like `import('./delayed.js')`.

**Purpose:**
- Loads third-party tag manager scripts specified in the `launchUrls` config array
- Runs in the delayed phase after the page is fully interactive
- Should be called inside the same `setTimeout` as other delayed operations

tasks.title: Configure Adobe Experience Platform credentials
tasks.description:
Replace the placeholder values in the `webSDKConfig` object in `scripts/scripts.js`:

- **`datastreamId`** — Your Adobe Experience Platform datastream ID
- **`orgId`** — Your Adobe IMS Organization ID (format: `XXXXX@AdobeOrg`)

**Where to find these:** Adobe Experience Platform Data Collection UI under **Datastreams**.

**Important:** Without valid credentials, no analytics data or personalization will be sent to Adobe.

tasks.title: Configure personalization settings
tasks.description:
If you want Adobe Target personalization:

- Set `martechConfig.personalization` to `true`
- Optionally adjust `personalizationTimeout` (default: `3000ms`)

**Important:** Enabling personalization requires `martechEager()` to resolve before the first section renders to prevent flash of original content (FOOC).

If you do **not** need personalization, leave the default (`false`) and skip this task.

tasks.title: Configure Launch container URLs (optional)
tasks.description:
If you have existing Adobe Launch (Tags) containers:

- Add container script URLs to the `martechConfig.launchUrls` array
- These scripts load in the **delayed phase** via `martechDelayed()`

**Note:** This is only needed for backward compatibility with existing Launch tag rules. Skip this task if you have no existing Launch containers.

tasks.title: Merge plugin branch to main
tasks.description:
Review all changes on the `plugin/aem-martech` branch and merge into main via pull request.

**Pre-merge checklist:**
- [ ] `head.html` has the three preload/preconnect hints
- [ ] `scripts.js` imports martech functions and calls `initMartech` with config
- [ ] `loadEager` waits for personalization with `Promise.all`
- [ ] `loadLazy` awaits `martechLazy()`
- [ ] `loadDelayed` calls `martechDelayed()` in `setTimeout`
- [ ] `datastreamId` and `orgId` have been replaced with real values
